text: I couldn't get an appointment for three weeks, and even then the clinic was nowhere near a bus line, so I had to take a whole day off work.
The doctor actually explained everything clearly and I felt listened to for the first time in years, which honestly surprised me.
My copay went up again this year, so I've quietly been skipping the follow-up visits even though I know I shouldn't.
Front desk staff were dismissive when I asked about the sliding-scale fee, like the cost of care was something I should already understand.
Nobody ever called back about the referral; the wait time on the phone alone was forty minutes before I gave up.
